item.title: Nasova stays in Fiji as Newcastle move called off
item.description: Fijian star Joji Nasova will not be joining Newcastle next season after the Red Bulls agreed to release him from his contract. The 25-year-old wing, who won a sevens silver medal at the Paris Olympics, had signed to move to the ambitious Prem club but will now stay with Fijian Drua.
item.content:encoded:
Fijian star Joji Nasova will not be joining Newcastle next season after the Red Bulls agreed to release him from his contract.

The 25-year-old wing, who won a sevens silver medal at the Paris Olympics, had signed to move to the ambitious Prem club but will now stay with Fijian Drua.

A club statement put the collapse of the deal down to “personal reasons”.

“While we were very much looking forward to welcoming Joji to Newcastle Red Bulls, we fully understand and respect the unique personal circumstances that have led to this decision,” Red Bulls general manager Neil McIlroy told the club wesbite. , external

“Contractual certainty is vital for clubs, players and the wider game. But at the same time rugby is built on people, families and communities, and we believe our responsibility as an employer extends beyond the field.

“We wish Joji, his family and the Fijian Drua every success for the future.”

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Curbing yellow and red cards pays off for MGM ruggers
item.description: MENDING weaknesses in their level of discipline paid off for the Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School under-16 Deans rugby squad, as they defeated Suva Grammar School 18-11 in the Southern Zone semi-finals last Saturday. This was pointed out by team captain Waisiki Ramatakubou, who shared the team began the week with an intent of minimising the number of yellow and red cards shown, which paid off h
item.content:encoded:
MENDING weaknesses in their level of discipline paid off for the Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School under-16 Deans rugby squad, as they defeated Suva Grammar School 18-11 in the Southern Zone semi-finals last Saturday.

This was pointed out by team captain Waisiki Ramatakubou, who shared the team began the week with an intent of minimising the number of yellow and red cards shown, which paid off handsomely in the end.

“We focused on our discipline in light of how we performed last week; there were many disciplinary cases (issues) noted such as two red cards shown,” Ramatakubou said.

“It was a reckless thing to show on our part.”

However, he said his teammates were pleased no cards were shown to them in their semi-final clash.

“But today (Saturday), we were happy that no red or yellow cards were given out to us today (Saturday).

“Other areas of our performance such as our defence and our attack were carried out very well.”

Grammar were stunned by the MGM defence and the set-pieces executed by the young ruggers of Vatuwaqa, Suva, as they broke through the tough Lions defence to make their mark felt.

The MGM U16 squad this year is made up of last year’s U15 squad, which lost 27-24 to Grammar in the 2025 Vodafone Deans national finals, held at the HFC Bank Stadium in Suva.

Saturday’s win saw them avenge themselves for last year’s loss, where they settled as runners-up.

In light of this, Ramatakubou said the team was thankful for the win earned and set their sights on the final this Saturday against Nasinu Secondary School.

The victory over the young lions of Veiuto means MGM books a spot in the national quarter-finals of the 2026 Vodafone Deans competition.

Meanwhile, Nasinu had beaten Marist Brothers High School 16-15 to meet MGM this week.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Naisewa’s double feat
item.description: McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women loosehead prop Karalaini Naisewa celebrated one of the biggest moments of her rugby career reaching her 30th cap in style as the Drua defeated the Queensland Reds Women 31-5 in their Super Rugby Women’s home semi-final at Churchill Park in Lautoka yesterday. The experienced front-rower described the occasion as a blessing, admitting she never imagined she would one day a
item.content:encoded:
McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women loosehead prop Karalaini Naisewa celebrated one of the biggest moments of her rugby career reaching her 30th cap in style as the Drua defeated the Queensland Reds Women 31-5 in their Super Rugby Women’s home semi-final at Churchill Park in Lautoka yesterday.

The experienced front-rower described the occasion as a blessing, admitting she never imagined she would one day achieve the milestone in the Drua jersey.

“It’s very special. I wasn’t expecting to reach these 30 caps, but I want to thank God for giving me this talent,” Naisewa said.

“Without Him I couldn’t make it this far. I also thank the girls, my family and the management for trusting me to do the work.”

The occasion became even more memorable when Naisewa scored the second try in the first half with a five-pointer.

She said running onto the field, her only focus was ensuring her 30th appearance would end in a win.

“It just came to my mind that my 30 caps needed to be special to me,” she said.

“Without the girls it wouldn’t be special. Thank you to the girls for this moment. It was a very special 30 caps for me and scoring that try made it even more special.”

Despite the convincing performance, Naisewa believes there is still work to be done before the home grand final.

She identified defence, tackle execution and set-piece accuracy as areas the team must improve if they are to lift the title next weekend.

“I think it’s just our defence going into the final and some of our tackling and set piece, so we’ll brush off the semi-final, learn from our mistakes this week and make it a good final next week,” she said.

Naisewa dedicated both the victory and her personal milestone to her family, thanking them for their unwavering support throughout her rugby journey.

She also acknowledged the passionate Drua supporters who packed Churchill Park, saying their energy played a significant role in the team’s success.

“I dedicate this win to my family for always supporting me and to the fans for coming out in numbers,” she said.

“Without them we couldn’t make it this far. Next week, come out in numbers again and support the girls.”

Naisewa will be hoping her memorable 30th cap becomes the perfect steppingstone towards adding a Super Rugby Women’s title to an already unforgettable week.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Unbeaten Lelean march into U18 zone finale
item.description: LELEAN Memorial School extended their unbeaten run in this year’s Eastern Zone Under-18 Deans competition with a convincing 29-0 victory over St. John’s College in the semi-finals on Saturday. Lelean produced another composed and clinical display, dominating both sides of the ball to keep their title aspirations firmly on track.
item.content:encoded:
LELEAN Memorial School extended their unbeaten run in this year’s Eastern Zone Under-18 Deans competition with a convincing 29-0 victory over St. John’s College in the semi-finals on Saturday.

Lelean produced another composed and clinical display, dominating both sides of the ball to keep their title aspirations firmly on track.

The Davuilevu-based side has been one of the standout teams throughout the season, and Saturday’s performance was another reminder of why they remain unbeaten this season.

From the opening whistle, Lelean controlled possession, won the physical battle and defended with discipline, denying St. John’s any opportunity to get on the scoreboard.

Their forward pack laid a solid platform with powerful carries and clean work at the breakdown, while the backline capitalised on attacking opportunities to stretch the defence and add points throughout the contest.

Equally impressive was Lelean’s defence, which stood tall whenever St. John’s threatened. The side’s organisation and commitment ensured they completed the match without conceding a single point, capping off one of their most dominant performances of the campaign.

The result not only secured Lelean a place in the Eastern Zone final but also preserved their perfect record heading into the biggest match of the season.

Lelean shifts its attention to the final, where they will look to complete an unbeaten Eastern Zone campaign and a chance to claim the prestigious Lutubula Trophy.

They will meet Ratu Kadavulevu School in the final this Saturday.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Poor finish | Seruvakula criticises impact players after third defeat
item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijians head coach Senirusi Seruvakula has openly admitted his bench players failed to make the impact required as Scotland stormed back from a 17-7 halftime to claim a 33-17 victory in their third round of Nations Cup Championship clash at the Scottish Gas Murrayfield in Edinburgh yesterday. After producing one of their best opening halves of the campaign, Fiji appeared on cours
item.content:encoded:
FIJI Water Flying Fijians head coach Senirusi Seruvakula has openly admitted his bench players failed to make the impact required as Scotland stormed back from a 17-7 halftime to claim a 33-17 victory in their third round of Nations Cup Championship clash at the Scottish Gas Murrayfield in Edinburgh yesterday.

After producing one of their best opening halves of the campaign, Fiji appeared on course for a memorable away win before Scotland dominated the final 40 minutes, with their replacements helping swing the momentum decisively in the hosts’ favour.

Seruvakula said the halftime message was straightforward – continue what had worked in the opening spell while maintaining discipline.

“The same thing that we were doing in the first half, keep it up, but maintain discipline and work for each other,” he said.

Instead, Fiji lost their grip on the contest.

“It didn’t work for us. The boys lost concentration, gave away penalties and sometimes we were casual.

“Full credit to Scotland. They really played well and they deserved the win.”

Seruvakula pointed to the contribution from the reserves as the defining difference between the two sides.

“We prepared well and we showed that in the first half, but our bench was not good. They did not come and lift the game and that’s what the last 20 minutes showed,” Seruvakula said.

While Fiji struggled to maintain intensity, Scotland introduced experienced internationals from the bench, including British and Irish Lions representatives, who helped keep the pressure on until fulltime.

“Their bench was good. They carried the momentum of the game till the end,” Seruvakula said.

“That’s why they never let down their pace.”

Despite the defeat, Seruvakula believed the Flying Fijians had shown significant improvement after last week’s disappointing loss 73-8 to England.

“We revealed our game last week against England,” he said.

“We had a good week. We prepared well and we showed that in the first half.”

The result leaves Fiji with plenty to reflect on, particularly the need for stronger finishes and greater impact from the replacements.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Delta Tigers overpower Nadroga
item.description: STRATUM Rewa FC delivered a clinical performance, thrashing Coastal Rental Cars/MMM Brothers Nadroga 6-0 at the HFC Bank Stadium yesterday in their Extra Premier League match. The Delta Tigers started slowly, but Josaia Sela broke the deadlock in the 28th minute of the first half. Just three minutes later, Asivorosi doubled Rewa’s lead, before Don Keana ended the first half on a high note, scoring
item.content:encoded:
STRATUM Rewa FC delivered a clinical performance, thrashing Coastal Rental Cars/MMM Brothers Nadroga 6-0 at the HFC Bank Stadium yesterday in their Extra Premier League match.

The Delta Tigers started slowly, but Josaia Sela broke the deadlock in the 28th minute of the first half. Just three minutes later, Asivorosi doubled Rewa’s lead, before Don Keana ended the first half on a high note, scoring in the final minute.

Rewa came out stronger after the break, continuously testing Nadroga’s defence.

Despite some poor execution early on, they finally found their rhythm, with Keana scoring his second and the team’s fourth goal in the 66th minute.

Epeli Valevou and Zafir Ali capped off the emphatic victory with goals in the 85th and 88th minutes respectively.

Head coach Priyant Manu praised his side’s performance and determination.

“The boys knew what was at stake and the boys really stood up today (yesterday) in the match,” he said.

“We had a slow start but after the water break, we started to pick up momentum and started to score goals finishing the game strong and this is what we hope to do in the coming games.”

The victory provides excellent momentum ahead of the OFC club competition.

“This was a good platform to play in really good ground condition, so the boys adapted in the second half, and we did what we came to do,” Manu explained.

Despite the dominant display, Manu is focused on improving the slow start before their next fixture against Nadi.

“It will be a tough task for us playing Nadi at home, but the message is simple: recover well, prepare well and come up with a good game plan against Nadi,” he said.

Other results: Ba FC edged Navua FC 1-0, Nasinu FC narrowly defeated Lautoka FC 2-1, and Tailevu Naitasiri secured a 1-0 win over Nadi.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Jasper defends Raluve zone title for national spot
item.description: JASPER Williams High School has successfully defended their West Secondary School Rugby Union U18 Raluve title after defeating Natabua High School 20-10 at Churchill Park in Lautoka on Saturday. The match served as the curtain raiser to the McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women and Queensland Reds Women semi-final.
item.content:encoded:
JASPER Williams High School has successfully defended their West Secondary School Rugby Union U18 Raluve title after defeating Natabua High School 20-10 at Churchill Park in Lautoka on Saturday.

The match served as the curtain raiser to the McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women and Queensland Reds Women semi-final.

In a closely contested encounter, Jasper Williams crossed the try line four times through Lusi Gonewai, Kalesi Solei, Emily Cevalawa and Adi Kelerayani.

Natabua High School fought hard throughout the match and managed to score two tries through Esther Tui and Aliti Ratudradra, but it was not enough to stop the defending champions.

Jasper Williams captain Ledua Grace credited their success to teamwork, humility and their faith.

“I would like to thank God for helping us win this match,” Grace said.

“Our main message was to stay humble, work hard and make sure we work together. We needed to stay connected and leave everything to God.”

The victory confirms Jasper Williams’ dominance in the West U18 Raluve competition as they continue their push for another successful season.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Ba Pro in top 16 pool
item.description: A RESILIENT defensive display earned Ba Provincial Freebird Institute a 13-3 victory over Ratu Navula College in their Fiji Secondary Schools Rugby Union Western Zone under-18 round-robin clash at Lawaqa Park in Sigatoka last Saturday. The win ensured Ba Provincial retained second place in the Western Zone standings, securing automatic qualification to the national U18 top 16 playoffs.
item.content:encoded:
A RESILIENT defensive display earned Ba Provincial Freebird Institute a 13-3 victory over Ratu Navula College in their Fiji Secondary Schools Rugby Union Western Zone under-18 round-robin clash at Lawaqa Park in Sigatoka last Saturday.

The win ensured Ba Provincial retained second place in the Western Zone standings, securing automatic qualification to the national U18 top 16 playoffs.

Head coach Krishant Reddy described the contest as a game of two contrasting halves, with his side dominating the opening spell before being forced to withstand relentless pressure after the break.

“It was a game of two halves,” Reddy said.

“In the first half we were more dominant with our attack, and in the second half Ratu Navula came at us strongly.

“We were very happy that our boys stayed strong on defence.

“It’s not easy to play a game of rugby without the ball. We have been reminding the boys about the importance of working hard without possession and I thought they responded really well.”

The 13-3 victory was a timely morale booster for Ba Provincial after Natabua High School beat them earlier.

“We had a tough game last week against Natabua and unfortunately we lost,” Reddy said.

“Our goal coming here was to get the win, secure our place in the top four and build momentum going into the national top 16 playoffs.”

While pleased with the result, Reddy said there was still work to do before the knockout stages.

“There are a couple of simple things we need to improve, especially our ball carry into contact and adapting to different playing conditions,” he said.

“Sometimes we play in windy conditions and how we adjust to those conditions will be crucial in the national top 16.”

Reddy also paid tribute to the team’s parents, describing their support throughout the campaign as one of the squad’s greatest strengths.

“Our biggest strength is our parents,” he said.

“They have been with us throughout our preparation, helping with nutrition and hydration and travelling with us to every game. We are thankful we were able to put a smile on their faces.”

Ba Provincial will now shift its focus to the national U18 top 16 play-offs, carrying valuable confidence from a gritty 13-3 victory.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
